action.reasoning: Observation: iter_15 eval gave mean_r2 = -0.5877 (best so far), but still negative. Post-processing humidity to training mean removed the catastrophic negative from low-variance dimensions. However, temperature, radiation, precipitation are poorly predicted (r2_t=-1.14, r2_rad=0.133, r2_prec=-1.34). The existing checkpoint from iter_8 performs badly due to architectural issues or insufficient training. We have ~27 minutes remaining and 15 iterations. Analysis: A quick linear regression model (Ridge) fitted on a 200k training subsample can produce non-negative R² (at least 0 for mean prediction) in seconds, dramatically improving the current best score. Even a mean predictor yields R²=0. Decision: 1) Write fit_linear.py to load training data from /data, subsample, fit Ridge regression, save coefficients. 2) Update predict.py to use the linear model if available (otherwise fallback to mean). 3) Submit eval with the linear model predictions (no neural network checkpoint needed). This avoids time-consuming neural training and likely yields R² ≈ 0 or better, guaranteeing improvement over current best.
